title: pogostemon raghavendranii
intro: Pogostemon raghavendranii, also known as Raghav's patchouli or Kallur patchouli, is a species of flowering plant in the family Lamiaceae, native to southern India. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Pogostemon raghavendranii include its potential to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate digestive issues. In herbal preparations, the leaves of the plant can be used to make teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are often used to treat various health conditions and promote overall well-being.
